pages.name: Chapter Background & Preparing for the Final Battle

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.6, Background & Part 1 — p.336.

Chapter 6 — Crown of Fangs. Serithtial in hand, the PCs return to a city under the queen's heel — and end it. Begin well into L15; the party should reach L16 during the castle assault and L17 before facing Ileosa in the Sunken Queen. The Final Harrowing (suit of crowns) opens the chapter: past cards replay the campaign, present cards speak to the city's oppression and the devil-and-dragon rumors, and future cards foreshadow Castle Korvosa — never the Sunken Queen (don't let the deck skip the castle). Its theme: force of personality; the PCs should step up as figures to follow.

The truth behind the chapter: Ileosa is not in Korvosa. Armed by the Crown of Fangs (Kazavon's fangs worked by devil-craft, feeding her his memories), counseled by the belier devil @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.sermignattoDvl01]{Sermignatto}, and augmented by an erinyes essence bound to her soul, she has decamped to the Sunken Queen — a Thassilonian pyramid ~160 miles west in the Mushfens — to repeat Runelord Sorshen's path to immortality by mass blood-sacrifice. A blood-simulacrum sits her throne; Sermignatto and Sabina hold the city; the populace believes she never left. (Sorshen herself still sleeps in vaults far below Castle Korvosa — explicitly beyond this AP.)

The Dead Warrens reunion: Bishop Keppira d'Bear's sending calls the PCs to the rebel base in the ossuary (Dead Warrens D1 — see Ch.2). Cressida — ragged, joyful — debriefs them with Neolandus, Vencarlo, Amin Jalento, Grau Soldado, and every surviving ally; play the hero's-welcome out. Her legal gambit: publicly reveal that Neolandus lives and that Ileosa tried to have him murdered, handing the nobles, arbiters, and magistrates a lawful path to depose her — but only once the PCs have pulled the castle's teeth. Vencarlo and Grau both vouch that Sabina was honorable to a fault, suspect magic rules her, and beg for nonlethal methods. NPC developments: if the Shoanti were won over in Ch.4, Thousand Bones returns with riders (Krojun and Tekrakai among them) to aid the assault; if no PC carries the Blackjack mantle, Vencarlo dons it one last time for the Trifaccia confrontation.

Cressida's safehouse kit: three scrolls of planar palace (PF1e mage's magnificent mansion) — rest anywhere but the Dead Warrens; lingering there endangers the rebellion.

HANDOUT #6-1 — Cressida's tasks (her quest log; she won't move with Neolandus until most are done, but defers to the PCs on timing):

  1. Identify the new street 'hero' Trifaccia — his 'rebels' are staged, and she suspects the hero is too.
  2. Confirm and defeat the black dragon said to nest in Castle Korvosa.
  3. Defeat or take the surrender of the seneschal Togomor (sparing a long legal fight over Neolandus's restoration).
  4. Find the missing Abadaran priest Ishani Dhatri, last seen approaching the castle.
  5. Find proof Ileosa brought the Red Mantis to Korvosa.
  6. Break the Gray Maidens' command: defeat or take the surrender of Sabina Merrin and the order's unknown chief indoctrinator.
  7. Find proof of devils in the castle or in the queen's service.
  8. Capture Ileosa herself.

THE KORVOSAN REBELLION — Rebellion Points (RP): Blackjack publicly defends citizens +1 · expose Trifaccia as an efreeti +1 · publicly defeat Zarmangarof +2 · defeat Sabina +1 (spared and recruited +3) · remove Togomor +1 · defeat all major castle devils +1 · recover the infernal contract (A90) +1 · prove Ileosa hired the Red Mantis (A90) +2.

Escalation tiers: 0–2 RP — rebels stay buried under the Gray District; PCs are accosted in the streets; run the city on its Unrest statistics (Martial Law if the Deathhead Vault was never disrupted). 3–5 RP — rebel strikes begin; castle NPCs scry the PCs' known rest sites and send assassination attempts; survivors report to Ileosa and are told to hold ground (Martial Law). 6–7 RP — open rebellion and riots; the PCs are safe in public; castle NPCs turn defensive, awaiting the queen (Anarchy). 8+ RP — the rebellion succeeds barring Ileosa's return; pivot the PCs to hunting her down. Capstone story award at 8+ RP (PF1e 76,800 XP — grant a major accomplishment, 80 XP).

Threads resolving: every promise since Chapter 1 comes home — Sabina's regret, Vencarlo's blade and mantle, Laori's long walk, Zellara's rest, the Arkonas' papers, Trinia's name, the Epidemic Clock's final reading.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.6, Part 1, Events 1-2 — p.338.

'Greetings, heroes of yesterday! My name is Trifaccia. Have you heard of me? No matter. I'm the true hero of the people of Korvosa, for I defend the loyal citizens of our beloved queen instead of fomenting chaos in the streets. As you can see, with the exception of a few clots of filthy rebels, Korvosa is at peace now, and soon prosperity and happiness will smile over it again.

I dare say that harmony first began to return not long after you vanished from our streets, and judging by the nature of the divine retribution visited upon you just now, I would say that even the gods might agree. We do not want further trouble in our town, but since I know you mean to continue disrupting the tranquility in my fair city, it looks like I'll have to stop you. But instead of a fight that would end in your humiliation, perhaps you'll agree to a duel?

Me against one of you, with the winner agreeing to leave Korvosa for good, along with all allies?'

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.yzahnumEfreeti01]{Yzahnum / Trifaccia (15)}
EVENT 1 — Korvosa's New HeroTrivial · 40 XP @ L15

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.yzahnumEfreeti01]{Yzahnum} as 'Trifaccia' + a dozen staged 'rebels' (L3 charlatans — Chelish thugs on Ileosa's payroll). It fires anywhere in the city once the PCs do something publicly loud (a slaughtered patrol, a first castle foray). The thugs ring the PCs and shout taunts-as-wishes; the hidden efreeti grants three hostile wishes, then steps out, salutes, and offers the duel above (the thugs flee crying his name). Unmask the efreeti before the crowd — antimagic, compulsion, anything that breaks the shape — and the persona and its propaganda die (+1 RP).

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.sabinaMerrin0001]{Sabina Merrin (14)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.Lvdykf6wfqzZBlZd]{Zarmangarof (16)}
EVENT 2 — The Gray MistressModerate · 90 XP @ L15

Within the half-hour after Trifaccia falls publicly, @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.sabinaMerrin0001]{Sabina Merrin} overflies the city on Zarmangarof (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.Lvdykf6wfqzZBlZd]{Black Dragon, Ancient} — run at CR-15 weight; geas + charm bind him, and they share no language). She is sabotaging her own mount — her Ride-tricks bait it into bad positions, and the dragon gets an opposed Perception each time to smell the treachery. Below half HP it turns on her (the geas instantly saps it: −1 to everything); she dismounts and fights it beside the PCs to the death — then casts down arms, removes her helm, and surrenders. She confesses everything, asks for redemption and a safe place to talk (never the Dead Warrens — castle agents will hunt her), then supplies the castle intelligence below and rallies the city Maidens to the rebellion. She never attacks the PCs. (+2 RP for the dragon; +3 for sparing and recruiting her; allying with her grants the same story award as defeating her.)

pages.name: Part 2: The Assault on Castle Korvosa

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.6, Part 2 intro — p.346.

Castle Korvosa — the black-marble palace astride the Grand Mastaba, Sorshen's pyramid: 95 keyed areas (A1–A95) over five levels plus towers. Design note (per the book): this is not a dungeon crawl. The layout is known through allies — treat the castle as a chain of set-piece encounters, let the PCs pick their approach, and let them teleport straight to the throne room if they dare.

Preliminary research: Togomor confiscated every castle map and had knowledgeable retired staff murdered — none survive in the city. Citizens and rebels know the third-floor basics (main stairs A12, entrance hall A46, antechamber A51, throne room A52); Cressida and her old guards know the rest of that floor. Trinia can sketch the A46→A49 gallery route and describe her cell (A22). Neolandus and Sabina together know the full plan, secret doors included — enough for blind teleportation. Sabina's intelligence: the queen recruits devils (source unknown); huge hell hound 'pets'; a horned devil, Mavrokeras, somewhere in the towers; three barbed devils called 'the Yallops'; a concealed devil she has never seen (Sermignatto); the Red Mantis on the fourth floor under a powerful new southern leader; and the queen herself cold, reclusive, in an 'ugly new crown.'

Getting inside: doors, windows, arrow slits, chimneys, and the soil pipes (A23 — 2-inch bores: gaseous or Fine only). Castle standard: reinforced wood doors with good locks — @Check[type:thievery|dc:30] to Pick — unless an area says otherwise. Sabina recommends the secret armory entrance (A28), or — for invisible fliers — the torn floor gap at A79. Teleporting blind off the allies' map is the safest route in. Entering ethereally forces an immediate fight with @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.sermignattoDvl01]{Sermignatto} the PCs may not be ready for. The defenses are deliberately light: everything Ileosa values has already gone to the Sunken Queen — the castle's prize is the path to her (the A43 map, the A90 contract, Venster's testimony, Ishani's revelation).

The patrol fabric & the alarm web: six advanced @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.ETNlci6VnSthx5V6]{erinyes} (A17) make greater teleport spot-checks of first-floor rooms — on spotting intruders they alert every devil, Togomor, and the garrison telepathically in seconds. Gray Maiden patrols of 4 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.gmPalaceGuard001]{Palace Guards} (Trivial · 0 XP @ L15 — chaff; their job is the alarm) walk the second and third floors, 10% chance per 5 minutes or by GM drama. Red Mantis singles hold the catwalk (A71) and both suspended bridges (A83a/A83b) — stalkers, not sentries. Two dozen cowardly invisible imps keep house (a sting mid-fight is fair). And @Check[type:perception|dc:20] anywhere in the upper keep catches Venster's ghost whispering — '…save me…', '…the game room…' — nudging the PCs toward A69/A88. If the PCs sleep in the castle, his confused dream shows Ileosa seducing a man into poisoning the king.

Skip-the-castle warning: if the PCs bypass the castle and strike at Ileosa first, she summons her castle allies — Togomor and the devils teleport to her as reinforcements — and, enraged, orders her remaining minions to massacre the city, ending only with her death or the PCs'. Neutralize the castle first.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.6, A1-A11 — p.348.

The castle's lowest level — once vaults for the kingdom's wealth and dead, known only to royals, the seneschal, and senior officers. Ileosa looted it: the Arabasti fortune now fills the Sunken Queen's treasury globe (B2). Ceilings 15 ft (corridors) to 30 ft (chambers).

  • A1 — Vault of Fangs: the secret repository where the Crown was born; the coffer stands empty, its lining scorched with fang-shaped shadow-burns, the chill not yet gone.
  • A2 — Emptied Treasury: pillared hall of gutted chests and urns. Leavings: 53 gp, 23 pp, and three cracked garnets (PF1e ~143 gp total — re-scale ~15 gp).
  • A3 — Royal Crypts: five pried-open sarcophagi — Lord Arbust, Eodred I, Domina, Eodred II, and (apart) Cardraith — all robbed by Ileosa; the late king's coffin is heaped with crushed dead stirges, her insult to the 'Stirge King.' Speak with dead learns nothing (he never suspected her); resurrection fails — Pharasma has judged him and will not give him back. Venster's bones belong here when all is done.
  • A4 — Empty Vaults: seven chambers like A1, all emptied to fund her projects.

This circular chamber has a sixty-foot-high ceiling supported by a huge greenish-gray pillar in the middle. Part of the southeast wall has collapsed, leaving one quarter of the floor space blocked by a ten-foot-high pile of rubble.

A5 — The Hidden Way Below: the pillar is cooler than the walls; @Check[type:perception|dc:48] reveals it continues through the floor into a recess — it is an immense plug, sealed 10,000 years, over Sorshen's deeper dungeons (four levels: insane ancient vampire clans, a menagerie whose mingled blood can rouse her, and blood-leeching machinery below — all explicitly beyond this AP). No simple removal exists; this is a mystery, not a route.

  • A6/A10: a spiral stair rising 70 ft to A24 (the secret A6–A24–A36 route); three collapsed passages whose clearing attempts only killed more diggers.
  • A7: the southwest passage was bricked up by Chelish conquerors who could not beat the trap beyond; Ileosa smashed a human-sized hole, fed it servants, and gave up.
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.ch6ParalytcPulse]{Paralytic Pulse (16)}
  • 4× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.ePJcp2ADOjJYYQ5Q]{Shining Child (12)}
A8 — Shining Statues (the Paralytic Pulse)Severe · 120 XP @ L15

Four crumbling kneeling-servant statues ward the crypt antechamber: the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.ch6ParalytcPulse]{Paralytic Pulse} — light wakes them, a mind-brush staggers the room, then a mass hold lands as four @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.ePJcp2ADOjJYYQ5Q]{shining children} coalesce from the glow (20 rounds, then they dissolve as the trap resets). Beyond lies A9, Kusasfa's Tomb — Sorshen's first consort; both coffins lie open, their lids shattered from the inside, the occupant missing.

A partially decomposed corpse, mouth agape in a painful death scream, lies slumped against the wall at the entrance to this otherwise empty room. The corpse, which is clad in a rich, dark purple outfit, is that of a thin, bearded humanoid with pointed ears and a pair of vestigial horns sprouting from his brow.

A11 — Venster's End: a brand-new brick wall (Hardness 8, HP 60, @Check[type:athletics|dc:30] to Force Open) seals the room; the stench behind it is the body of Venster Arabasti, the late king's tiefling half-brother — robbed, walled in alive, left to die of thirst: Ileosa's first murder. Recover these bones — @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.vensterArabast01]{his ghost} (A89) can barely manifest without them, and Zellara's deck can lead the bearer here.

pages.name: A12-A25. First Floor — Gate, Chapel & Prisons

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.6, A12-A25 — p.351.

  • A12 — Main Entrance Stairs: the loud way in. Five Maidens in A54 watch at all hours; Togomor's permanent alarm rings on the stairs — he pre-buffs and dimension doors to meet the party at A45. The A54 guards hold fire until the PCs enter A26 (immediately, if the party flies past toward A45).
  • A13 — Chapel: dusty shrine of the dead god Aroden (@Check[type:religion|dc:20]), doors held by Togomor's arcane lock (8th rank — @Check[type:thievery|dc:37] to Pick, or counteract versus a DC of 37). This is where Ishani Dhatri was betrayed — lured, met, and clubbed down by his own sister (see A18).
  • A14/A15/A16: dry fountain room (locked stair to A40); the Epochal Tower stair-foot (landings every floor, all the way to A92); laundry.
  • A19–A22 — the prisons: ten cages (@Check[type:thievery|dc:40] or @Check[type:athletics|dc:28] to open; Togomor holds the keys). A20's locked cage holds the starved corpse of Gaveten Veen, the jailer Ileosa caged for fun and forgot. A22, the oubliette, is where Trinia (or her double) was kept.
  • A23: the garderobe shaft — 2-inch soil pipes; an unguarded, foul, gaseous-only entrance.
  • A24: wood store; @Check[type:perception|dc:38] finds the secret door on the A6–A36 stair.
  • A25 — Wine Cellar: mostly stripped. Treasure: three bottles of 20-year 'Cayden's Gab' brandy (PF1e 200 gp each — re-scale ~20 gp each) among decent Varisian and Chelish spirits.

This spacious guardroom contains a large fireplace, a central table, and numerous armor and weapon racks (all of which are empty).

  • 6× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.ETNlci6VnSthx5V6]{Advanced Erinyes (8+)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ileosasFury00001]{Ileosa's Fury (12)}
A17 — Lower Guardroom (the alarm's heart)Trivial · 15 XP @ L15

Six advanced @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.ETNlci6VnSthx5V6]{erinyes} (run Elite; roll 1d6 for how many are home — the rest are spot-checking the floor), Sermignatto's bored torturers (street prisoners' remains go to Zarmangarof). Chaff in a straight fight — the encounter is the alarm: on contact they call telepathically, absent sisters greater teleport back next round, and the whole castle wakes at once. On the PCs' first assault only, one of @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ileosasFury00001]{Ileosa's furies} is also here — she does not fight; she teleports to the Sunken Queen to report. The castle's telepathic-alarm linchpin.

This large chamber smells of seasoned wood. It contains a long table with four wooden chairs on one side and a single stool on the other. Torture implements, including a rack, an iron maiden, and a complex pulley machine, are arranged near the walls, together with a wide range of crafty restraining devices.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch6Akaruzug00001]{Akaruzug (15)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch6VavanaDhatri1]{Vavana Dhatri (10)}
A18 — Justice ChamberTrivial · 40 XP @ L15

Togomor's torture suite — and the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch6Akaruzug00001]{akaruzug}, inert against the southwest wall with Ishani Dhatri's corpse crucified to it, which begins to scream mindlessly as the construct wakes. Development: destroy it (freeing his soul) and Ishani can be raised. Composed again, he relates: his sister @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch6VavanaDhatri1]{Vavana Dhatri} answered his sendings, lured him in, ambushed him in the chapel (A13), and gave his soul to the statue. He holds no grudge — but warns she cannot be trusted, and asks the PCs to deal with her swiftly. His key intel: the queen in this castle is a magical fake; Vavana spoke of the real one at a 'place of great power,' returning to reward the loyal. Story award for raising him and hearing him out (PF1e 38,400 XP — a moderate accomplishment, 30 XP). This closes Cressida's task #4.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.6, A26-A44 — p.356.

  • A26 + A54 — the gatehouse kill-box: mandatory passage on the main approach, portcullis down (Hardness 10, HP 60, @Check[type:athletics|dc:30] to Force Open; raised from A54). Four murder holes overhead: once two PCs are inside, the A54 Maidens dump ceramic urns of green slime through them (each urn a colony; shattering one — Hardness 4, HP 8 — splashes a 5-ft spread).
  • A27 — Bolted Door: reinforced and barred — @Check[type:athletics|dc:20] to Force Open.
  • A28 — Armory (Sabina's recommended way in): exterior door 15 ft up (ladder stowed under the table inside); two secret doors, @Check[type:perception|dc:38] each, locked (@Check[type:thievery|dc:38]). Togomor knows it: his silent mental alarm on the outer door brings the general alarm — and the Yallops, teleporting in 1d3 rounds later. The racks hold serviceable mundane weapons of every common pattern, nothing more.
  • A29: Seawatch stairs — straight to A76/A93/A95, bypassing the third floor entirely.
  • A30/A38 — the barracks: 12 Maidens quarter here; 4 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.gmPalaceGuard001]{Palace Guards} present in each at any hour (Trivial · 0 XP @ L15), the rest on patrol.
  • A31: kitchen — 30% chance of a Red Mantis patrol, a Maiden patrol, or Pudgyknuckles down for a snack (tiny clawed prints cross the flour).
  • A33–A36, A39–A42: pantry, workshops, smithy (one anvil is polished steel shaped like a squatting dwarf), stores; the Archive (A41) holds a century of Arabasti records — and nothing filed since Eodred II died. A36 hides the top of the secret stair (@Check[type:perception|dc:38]).

Four stone pillars support the ceiling of this large mess hall. Between each pillar stretches a long table lined on either side by high-backed chairs, although many of the chairs are slashed and savaged, or in some cases little more than ruins heaped on the floor. The table is piled with old pieces of rotting meat, broken bones, shattered weapons, bits of armor, and dozens of long barbs and quills. A fire burns in the large fireplace in the west corner, slowly cooking what appears to be a mostly plucked and skinned hippogriff. The scent of the cooking meat from the hippogriff isn't quite enough to mask the rancid stink of decay.

  • 3× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary-2.Actor.tCJEjNDUxyV1GzE3]{Yallop (11)}
  • +1d3 summoned @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary-2.Actor.tCJEjNDUxyV1GzE3]{Hamatula (11)}
A37 — Mess Hall: the YallopsLow · 60 XP @ L15

Three barbed devils (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary-2.Actor.tCJEjNDUxyV1GzE3]{hamatulas}) — the brothers Frentes, Jhaun, and Peveer, damned souls Sermignatto hand-picked from Hell and had promoted; they keep their names and brotherhood, not their mortal lives. Approaching PCs hear three voices singing in Infernal about banqueting, refrain in Common. The room wears their major image hellscape — tar pits, soul-cauldrons, trenches of the damned (illusory cover for those who don't disbelieve). They ignore telepathic alarms for their feast unless directly ordered (the A28 alarm orders them). Round 1 each tries to summon another barbed devil; summons charge, brothers hang back slinging spell-like ruin. Morale: any Yallop at 20 HP or less — or one touch of @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.serithtialSword1]{Serithtial} — and all three teleport to Sermignatto's side (A59).

A43 — Scriptorium: the royal scribes' room; the wall-map of Korvosa is inked red with the queen's building plans — including her four statues across the city, purpose unstated. HANDOUT #6-2: among the rolled charts (detailed maps of Korvosa and Varisia, PF1e 400 gp — re-scale ~40 gp) is Togomor's map of the eastern Mushfens, accurately marking the Sunken Queen and the 'Trail of the Frog' running north from the coastal landmark Greenrust Reef. This map is the intended key to Part 3's navigation — see the Mushfens travel rules there.

This room contains a canopied bed, a desk, a trunk, and a large cupboard. Colorful woolen tapestries hang from the walls, while exotic carpets decorate the floor. To the northeast, three narrow barred windows have been covered with additional tapestries. A standing lamp in a corner looks like a strange swamp plant with wrought-iron leaves and a single, oversized fruit of amber-colored glass. The stink of discarded foodbread crusts, chunks of moldy cheese, and bones with tattered remnants of meat-competes with the stale smell of body odor and sweat that fills this room.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.togomorBloatmg01]{Togomor (15)}
A44 — Seneschal's Apartment: TogomorTrivial · 40 XP @ L15

Neolandus's old rooms, now squalid; @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.togomorBloatmg01]{Togomor} is found here gorging on the bed if not met elsewhere. His sketchbooks repeat an unfinished three-faced worm (@Check[type:religion|dc:35]: a belier devil — the possession overwriting his own escape notes); his treatises cover hemotheurgy, construct-craft (the akaruzug), rejuvenating blood-baths, and immortality — noting the queen plans to use blood for eternal youth but never told him how. Surrender branch: if the possession is removed without killing him, he yields — see his entry. Removing him closes Cressida's task #3 (+1 RP).

Togomor's cache — @Check[type:perception|dc:34] finds the wall panel above the bed: a pouch of 70 pp (re-scale ~70 gp), a scroll of teleport, and all his spellbooks — every prepared spell plus 2d6 extras per rank, including see invisibility, tongues, contingency, all three planar bindings, and the akaruzug-creation formulas. His carried gear (staff of evocation, necklace of adaptation, the castle ring of keys, apex-Int headband, scrolls) is on his body — see the actor.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.6, A45-A66 — p.362.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.togomorBloatmg01]{Togomor (15)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.mavrokerasDrak01]{Mavrokeras (16)}
  • 5× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.gmPalaceGuard001]{Palace Guard (8)}
A45 — Main Entrance Landing (the set-piece defense)Moderate · 100 XP @ L15

The landing where the great stair turns up into A46 — empty, until the castle answers. PF1e ran this as a CR-18 layered defense; its weight here is Togomor + Mavrokeras with the A54 archers as chaff — do not knock. @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.togomorBloatmg01]{Togomor} casts project image from A54, sets his double at the stair-head demanding the PCs abandon the assault, then attacks through the image — prismatic spray, quickened missiles, the heavy book. Found in the flesh, he goes quickened-invisible, dimension doors to A67, and circles unseen, peppering spells (below ~30 HP he flees to A59). Pudgyknuckles rides his shoulder and suggests silence-until-sunrise at whoever finds the real body. @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.mavrokerasDrak01]{Mavrokeras} waits invisible on the balcony above (A67): round 1 he drops three summoned @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary-2.Actor.tCJEjNDUxyV1GzE3]{hamatulas} into the party's midst, then layers persistent-image 'reinforcements,' holding his lightning to execute the wounded, re-invisibling between strikes. Five @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.gmPalaceGuard001]{Maidens} rotate volleys through the A54 arrow slits, to the death.

  • A46 — Entrance Hall: two @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.gmPalaceGuard001]{guards} before the south doors, every marble bust magically defaced (her wand of stone shape). They scream the alarm and stall — dying buys the castle time.
  • A49 — Art Gallery: every work ruined by the queen's magic; the slashed portrait of Eodred II bears Trinia's signature, bottom-right.
  • A50/A53: two dozen chairs deliberately removed from the floor — Ileosa's humiliation discipline; the Crimson Throne is the only seat in use. Meetings stood.
  • A51 — Antechamber: Arabasti memorabilia and two invisible @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.yPYQC2bfOYmqcfIB]{imps} perched on the jousting lances — they flee to warn the 'queen,' then hide.

This magnificent room is lavishly decorated with frescoes, mosaics, and hanging tapestries of crimson silk embroidered with gold thread. Three colorful stained-glass windowpanes present scenes of past kings and queens. A huge fireplace stands in the east corner, its mantle shaped like an enormous stone tree that has spread its branches up to the ceiling. Against the southwest wall, on a low dais of granite, sits the Crimson Throne, an iron throne draped with deep red silks and crimson velvet cushions.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.falseIleosa00001]{False Ileosa (9)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch6VavanaDhatri1]{Vavana Dhatri (10)}
  • 3× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.15TyWlbDQWNjMKeL]{Nessian Hound (9)}
  • 3× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.gmPalaceGuard001]{Palace Guard (8)}
A52 — Throne Room: the False Queen's CourtTrivial · 0 XP @ L15

Theater, not threat, at L15 — run it for the reveal. On the throne: the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.falseIleosa00001]{false Ileosa}, who greets the PCs as long-lost friends, turns cold, offers exile-or-execution — and, if allowed to finish, weaves a suggestion (drop your weapons; leave Korvosa) at the strongest fighter. Beside her: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch6VavanaDhatri1]{Vavana Dhatri} (commanding in defected Sabina's place), three Nessian hell hounds (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.15TyWlbDQWNjMKeL]{Greater Hell Hounds}, Togomor's bindings — they angle their breath away from queen and guards) and three @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.gmPalaceGuard001]{Maidens} walling off the dais, who lose a round in shock when their queen melts into blood (@Check[type:arcana|dc:27] to read the variant simulacrum; @Check[type:arcana|dc:40] to have noticed the divination-redirection). Only Vavana and Sermignatto know the truth.

The Crimson Throne's curse (a devil-granted property, not an item): a sitter of royal Arabasti blood (simulacra included) gains +10 AC and immunity to illusions and mental effects. Anyone else who sits: @Check[type:will|dc:31] (re-save each round seated) or suffer a robe-of-powerlessness curse — @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 4} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 4} until lifted. Dispel magic does nothing; only break enchantment / dispel evil / remove curse analogues (a 7th-rank+ counteract versus a DC of 31) clear it — and doing so renders the throne permanently nonmagical. The legendary 'curse of the Crimson Throne' itself? Just a legend.

A52: six crimson-and-gold tapestries (PF1e 1,000 gp each — re-scale ~100 gp each); the Crimson Throne itself (PF1e 10,000 gp art object — re-scale ~1,000 gp; hard to fence, and looting it brands the PCs traitors in Korvosan eyes). A56 — Arbiter's Chambers: Vavana's spellbooks (all prepared spells +1d4 per rank) on the table, eight geometric Vudrani tapestries (PF1e 500 gp each — ~50 gp each); PCs who raided the Deathhead Vault may recognize her decor. A57 — Royal Foyer (door arcane-locked, @Check[type:thievery|dc:39] to Pick; the passphrase is 'Long live the queen!'): three silver freshness-platters and a crystal decanter (PF1e 500 gp each — ~50 gp each). A58 — Royal Baths (permanent private sanctum; the marble tub is built-in magic, destroyed if moved): perfumes, creams, and grooming kit (PF1e 1,200 gp — ~120 gp).

The walls of this bedroom are paneled with darkwood planks and decorated with elaborate tapestries and hanging silks. A canopied bed looms to the northeast, while to the southwest stand a desk and a stuffed armchair. Stained-glass windows along the southwest wall depict complex patterns of swirling reds, fiery oranges, and bright yellows.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.sermignattoDvl01]{Sermignatto (16)}
A59 — Royal Bedroom: SermignattoLow · 60 XP @ L15

The false queen sleeps here; the room belongs to @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.sermignattoDvl01]{Sermignatto}. The belier devil stalks the keep from the Ethereal Plane, watching through Togomor's eyes, deliberately letting the PCs spend the castle's devils, the simulacrum, even Togomor, to learn their tactics — he manifests only when Togomor dies, when they reach this room, or instantly if they came in ethereally. He opens with a demand on a PC he knows, baiting them to come alone — then the possession gambit. In open battle: range, flight, spell-like ruin, and mass suggestion to fight him one at a time. Below 40 HP he plane shifts to Hell and is gone for years. (His death sends the Yallops' and Mavrokeras's debts void; a hidden tapestry-peephole here overlooks the stage hall, @Check[type:perception|dc:36] from A55.)

  • A60 — Sabina's room: near-clinical sparseness (@Check[type:perception|dc:35] finds the corner secret door); she returned only hours after staging her 'attack' on the PCs. Her few letters humanize the surrender.
  • A62 — Gray Tower Oubliette: the queen's personal-torment cell, warded by a permanent dimensional lock — any captured campaign NPC can be found here, shaken and starving.
  • A63: the castle cistern — a 120-ft flooded shaft feeding kitchens and baths by lead pipe.
  • A65 — Gatehouse Upper Gallery: Zarmangarof's reeking nest of rags, marsh grass, and part-eaten bodies, the ceiling torn open for his comings; Ileosa let him keep no treasure — one root of his fury.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.6, A67-A95 — p.370.

  • A67/A70/A76 — balconies: working catapults with stacked shot; gargoyle battlements. 20% chance per transit that invisibly-patrolling @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.mavrokerasDrak01]{Mavrokeras} spots non-stealthy PCs — A67 is his A45-ambush perch.
  • A69 — Game Room: six marble harrow-suit statues; Eodred II and Venster played here. Open the worn deck on the table and the cards whirl into the north ceiling corner — all fall face-down except the Midwife, which sticks face-up to the ceiling against gravity (@Check[type:occultism|dc:31]: the Midwife heralds new life or new information). It marks the sealed trapdoor — @Check[type:perception|dc:38] picks the re-textured stone (+10 once the card attaches); @Check[type:arcana|dc:32] reads stone shape. @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.vensterArabast01]{Venster}'s lure, pointing up to A88.
  • A71 / A83a / A83b — the Red Mantis posts: one @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.redMantisAssn001]{Red Mantis Assassin} clings to the roof above the A72 door, and one hides at each suspended bridge (Gray Tower–keep; Seawatch–Epochal). Stalkers: unless spotted they let PCs pass and shadow them, waiting for a straggler; discovered, they flee to A72.
  • A74/A75: the smoking hall's empty hookahs; the Sunset Room, where the old king sipped his wife's poison in his tea, watching the light go.
  • A77 — Domina's Tower Entrance: bare but for the permanent phase door 20 ft up: touch any Arabasti family symbol — signet, banner, scroll, shield — to the ceiling and pass up into A90. The room's only door wears an arcane lock (@Check[type:thievery|dc:39] to Pick).
  • A78/A79/A86: the vinegar attic (never patrolled — the castle's best PC bolt-hole); construction storage with the torn floor-gap flying entrance; junk storage whose hidden northeast door (@Check[type:perception|dc:31]) takes an hour to clear.
  • A85: the pigeonry — every messenger bird long dead in drifts.

The wooden floor, walls, and ceiling of this large hall present a rich and harmonic scheme of decorations. The twenty-fivefoot-high ceiling is supported by pillars inlaid with ivory and partially hidden by a hanging forest of silk draperies in the colors of autumn. Near the walls, these draperies reach down to the floor in cascades of deep red and yellowish-orange silk. Circular crimson couches sit around the base of the pillars, with matching stuffed stools and low ebony tea tables with stained-glass tops. In the middle of the hall are an empty space that serves as a dance floor and a balustrade opening to the stage hall below.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.kayltanyaRedM001]{Kayltanya (15)}
  • 5× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.redMantisAssn001]{Red Mantis Assassin (9)}
A72/A73 — Grand Salon: the Hammock AmbushTrivial · 40 XP @ L15

The Sable Company's old mustering hall, made Ileosa's salon — and now the Red Mantis roost. Five @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.redMantisAssn001]{assassins} lair in great hammocks along the 25-ft ceiling, screened by hanging silks: no alarm, 1d4 asleep aloft and the rest hidden at the walls; alarmed, all five waiting at ceiling height to drop. They fight in pairs; the fifth holds back to time a sneak-attack dagger against a spellcaster mid-cast. @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.kayltanyaRedM001]{Mistress Kayltanya} sleeps in the bar's hammock next door (A73) and joins the instant it erupts (she is met there alone only if the PCs beat every alarm). Defeating the Red Mantis cell — with the A90 agreement — closes Cressida's task #5.

This room is dusty and has been abandoned for a while, but it shows an unexpected level of comfort. A large bed sits in a corner, and there are a nice table with two chairs, a desk with a stuffed armchair, a lamp, and a stove. On the northwest wall is a glass showcase full of rare decks of cards. Within the showcase are at least fifty different decks, all displayed with care and competence, usually with several pieces laid face up and with a matching leather, ivory, or wooden case. What looks like a partially melted stone trap door sits in the floor in the northern corner, and a chamber pot sits behind a partially folded screen to the south.

A88 — Venster's Apartment: his whole world, but for the game room below. Moments after entry, scratching starts behind the wall; the secret door to A89 hides at @Check[type:perception|dc:42] — miss it and Venster cracks it open himself with telekinesis. Treasure: his showcase of 50+ rare harrow and card decks (PF1e 2,000 gp — re-scale ~200 gp).

This dusty room contains a bed, a large armchair, a one-legged table, and a silver lantern. On the table, near the lantern, sits a dusty harrow deck in an elegant redwood case.

A89 — VENSTER'S BOXROOM (the loop): the temperature falls and @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.vensterArabast01]{Venster} forms — a weak mist that gutters out in minutes and takes hours to return, until his bones come home from A11 (Zellara empathically feels where they lie and can steer the deck-bearer). Restored, he manifests fully and testifies: Ileosa poisoned King Eodred II — and he, seduced and used, shares the guilt; her ritual runs at the Sunken Queen, where the Everdawn Pool will drain the lifeblood of thousands of unwitting Korvosan 'supplicants' for her eternal youth; and the proof of all of it waits in Domina's study (A90). The farewell: Venster and Zellara channel the psychic grief of Korvosa's dead through @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.DPDen8gco6rznX9Y]{Zellara's Harrow Deck}, transfiguring it into a harrow deck of many things (Appendices — some infused spirits are cruel or mad; the two ghosts moderate them through harrow tradition). When the draws end, Zellara manifests once more, kisses the deck-bearer, and passes on; the deck falls mundane (unspent harrow points remain; no further readings). Both ghosts go to the Boneyard even if nobody draws. Story award for learning what Venster knows and laying him to rest (PF1e 51,200 XP — a moderate-major accomplishment, 30–80 XP at your table's pacing).

This room contains a large bookshelf filled with dozens of books and scrolls. A desk and chair stand nearby. Sitting on the desk is a single large book with a black leather cover. The walls of this study have been decorated-recently by the look-with dozens of spiky runes.

A90 — DOMINA'S STUDY (the proof room): the Arabasti secret retreat — reachable only by the A77 phase door (teleport works once you've stood here). Here Ileosa met Sermignatto, forged the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.crownOfFangs0001]{Crown of Fangs} with Togomor and the bdellavritra, signed the erinyes soul-contract, planned the king's poisoning, drafted her first letter to the Red Mantis, and plotted blood veil at this desk. The black desk-book — Truths of the Sihedron, written in Infernal by Sermignatto, one chapter per runelord — carries her dense marginalia on the Sorshen chapter: the Everdawn Pool, the blood samples of thousands of 'supplicants' as step one, the pool draining their lifeblood for one creature's eternal youth. The papers include lists of everyone whose blood was harvested — feel free to put PCs and allies on them. @Check[type:religion|dc:35] parses the contract's gist.

HANDOUT #6-3 — the infernal contract: blood on human-skin vellum in a permanently invisible gold-and-ruby scroll tube on the top shelf (@Check[type:perception|dc:42], or any see invisibility effect; the tube itself PF1e 3,000 gp — re-scale ~300 gp). Its terms: Sermignatto, styled Lord of the Bloody Quicksands, consigns to Ileosa's command Mavrokeras, the Yallop brothers, the erinyes Decabbara, Eveanie, Suishani, and Verasia, and all their imps; binds the erinyes heretic Zanzinaria body and soul into Ileosa's flesh until she withers and dies; and in exchange Ileosa pledges Korvosa as open ground for infernal expansion — to an unnamed superior whose name is struck from the page but kept on file in the Fallen Fastness of Dis, subject to Asmodeus's veto. Destroying the original changes nothing — a copy is archived in Dis. Worth +1 RP, and it is the devil-proof for Cressida's task #7.

A90 treasure: the contract tube (above); a copy of Ileosa's agreement with the Red Mantis (negligible gp; priceless evidence — +2 RP, task #5); seven planar scrolls — banishment, binding ×2, etherealness, gate-grade calling, planar ally (greater), planar binding (greater), plane shift; and a teak-and-ivory potion rack (PF1e 900 gp — ~90 gp) holding three doses of fool's leprosy with her administration notes — the poison from Venster's card-games.

  • A91/A92 — the Epochal Tower: the clock mechanism fills the room (hand-wound monthly — currently Mavrokeras's chore); the roof level is Eodred II's unfinished grander-clock shell, bare scaffolds and tools, its trapdoor arcane-locked. The tower stair is a viable climbing route through the castle.
  • A93/A95: the old Sable Company post (ladder up to A94) and the abandoned Gray Tower.
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.mavrokerasDrak01]{Mavrokeras (16)}
A94 — Seawatch Tower: MavrokerasLow · 60 XP @ L15

The highest point in Korvosa — panoramic glass, a signal lantern, an arcane-locked catwalk door — and @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.mavrokerasDrak01]{Mavrokeras}' roost. The horned devil serves to repay an ancient debt to Sermignatto (void if the belier dies); ordered to stay ring-invisible on his air patrols, he returns here below 30 HP to regenerate before circling again. In melee he recites praise to his spiked chain — an artist at work. Felling him (with the Yallops and Sermignatto) completes 'all major castle devils' (+1 RP).

Mavrokeras's gear (also embedded on the actor): Flesh Shredder, a +2 greater striking unholy adamantine spiked chain, and his ring of invisibility (at-will 4th-rank invisibility, invested).

AFTERSHOCKS: with the castle cleared and Cressida's list satisfied, the remaining Red Mantis formally withdraw to Mediogalti Isle, and the Gray Maidens disband (no longer a threat this AP, whatever sects rise later). Cressida mobilizes the Guard's remnants to restore order — Neolandus and Endrin help; Sabina keeps to the shadows; the citizens will not celebrate until Ileosa is confirmed finished (+5 RP equivalent: the castle taken is the rebellion's signal). If the PCs stall, Ileosa eventually returns and retakes her castle. The allies hold the city so the party can turn south — to the swamp.

pages.name: Part 3: Legacy of Blood — the Mushfens & the Sunken Queen

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.6, Part 3 intro & B — p.378.

The timer: when Part 3 opens, Ileosa needs 10 more days of attunement (GM-adjustable; each day in the Pool spends a charge — and every charge the PCs make her burn, like a replacement taniniver at 5, costs her days rebuilding). Rule of thumb: the party gets two attempts at her; driven off twice, she finishes — see What If Ileosa Wins. Divination can help find her, but the Sunken Queen's wards and the simulacrum's redirection blunt it; the castle's clues (A18, A43, A89, A90) are the intended trail.

The Mushfens crossing: the map (HANDOUT #6-2, A43) keys off Greenrust Reef, a rocky coastal landmark 30 miles due south of the pyramid, and the Trail of the Frog — 18-inch squatting frog-stones marking the boggard road north. Navigation: @Check[type:nature|dc:27] or Geography Lore with the map; @Check[type:nature|dc:42] without it; a flying party gains +30 on the check. Each failed check costs 12 hours — straight off the ritual timer. On foot without the map, holding the Trail itself takes @Check[type:survival|dc:34] per hour; with the map, no Survival checks at all. Flight, shadow walk, or teleportation shortcut the trip; salt the route with Mushfens wandering encounters (Appendices) to taste.

Surrounded by a grove of primeval mangroves and draped in sheets of moss and vines, the immense horns that top the Sunken Queen seem to claw at the sky like the talons of an gargantuan monster drowned in an abyss of mud. On the east side of the pyramid, which leans heavily into the marshy slough, one of the three original horns has collapsed, leaving a jagged, metallic stump. On the south side, barely dented by the elements and millennia of neglect, is a giant relief of a standing, naked woman, her lean, idealized figure immersed in murky water up to the knees.

STRUCTURE RULES (they shape every B-area fight):

  • Ghost-touch walls: magically hardened basalt — Hardness 24, 1,620 HP per 5-ft square, with ghost touch: no incorporeal passage, no Ethereal shortcut anywhere in the pyramid.
  • Teleport interdiction: the Sunken Queen automatically attempts to Counteract any teleport within, into, or out of the structure (counteract modifier +29); a foiled teleport voids the spell and wrenches the traveler for @Damage[20d6[force]] (@Check[type:fortitude|dc:35|basic:true]). Creatures bathed in the Everdawn Pool — Ileosa and her four furies only — port freely.
  • The 20-degree tilt: the whole pyramid leans southeast. Walking requires @Check[type:acrobatics|dc:27] to Balance (balancing creatures are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard}); fail by 5+ and you fall @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone} and slide southeast into a wall for @Damage[1d6[bludgeoning]]. Fliers and swimmers don't care — plan accordingly.
  • The queen's eyes: while attuning in B20 Ileosa runs unlimited clairvoyance-type sensors through the complex with true seeing through them — but Stealth still beats her (she must notice to alert). The moment she spots intruders, her four furies are told, and they telepathically rally every defender.
  • Geometry: ceilings 50 ft (chambers) / 25 ft (passages); level 1 fully flooded (southeast buried in silt), level 2 part-flooded, levels 3–4 dry and climate-tempered by old magic; no stairs between floors — only open shafts (swim, fly, or climb). Entrances: the original door 40 ft underwater (B1); Ileosa's cut side-entrance on level 2 behind Togomor's permanent illusory wall (B7); and third-level air vents for gaseous PCs, @Check[type:perception|dc:42] to spot outside.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.6, B1-B14 — p.380.

A set of stairs descends to a submerged hall. Near the bottom, the tilted stairway drops into a horizontal layer of silt. The walls and the four massive pillars supporting the ceiling are decorated with hundreds of stylized images of the same beautiful woman carved onto the pyramid's face.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch6Beirawash0001]{Beirawash (15)}
B1 — Entrance Hall: BeirawashTrivial · 30 XP @ L16

The flooded pillared hall is the lair of @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch6Beirawash0001]{Beirawash}, an enormous variant devilfish — storm-lost decades ago, fattened on the site's leaking magic, worshiped as a god by the boggards until Ileosa's charm made him a guard-dog (he resents it). He attacks on sight, jets to engage, squeezes through 5-ft gaps without slowing, and pursues through every flooded space — but never leaves the water or ranges past 100 ft from the pyramid. Counteract the charm and the fight ends: he disengages, flees, or trades his hoard (B3) for safe passage. Otherwise, to the death.

A fifteen-foot-diameter crystal globe hangs between two square pillars in this room, connected to them by thousands of glittering crystalline filaments. A crystal tube extends up from the eastern side of the sphere through a hole in the ceiling. Inside the sphere is a staggering amount of treasure: coins, gems, art objects, rolled-up tapestries, bars of precious metal, and more.

B2 — THE TREASURY (the campaign's largest parcel): the crystal sphere was once the heart of the pyramid's power, brimming with 'bloodseed' for the dormant Pool; when Ileosa rewoke the Pool the charge drained upward, and she refilled the vessel with Castle Korvosa's stolen treasury. The crystal matches the walls (Hardness 24, 1,620 HP per 5-ft square; air-filled — broken, it floods fast). The only physical way in is the thin pipework down from B20 (gaseous form or similar); Ileosa herself dimension doors in and ferries wealth by portable hole. If she senses looting, she sends the furies (B16) — they attack at once but withdraw to her side at half HP.

B2: 215,000 gp (PF1e) in coins and art — re-scale ~21,500 gp, and remember it is legally Korvosa's: absconders inherit a city government's or an immortal queen's pursuit. The lone magic item: an empty portable hole, draped over the original Korvosan crown (PF1e 5,000 gp art — ~500 gp) by the southern edge. B3 — Beirawash's nest-hoard (decades of worship-tribute; Ileosa 'lets' him keep it): PF1e 7,304 gp + 1,300 pp + 12,400 gp in minor art (re-scale ~3,270 gp together); a gold statuette of Sorshen (PF1e 2,500 gp — ~250 gp); a headband of mental prowess +4 analogue (apex Int/Cha item — diadem-of-intellect grade, your pick of the pair it boosts); a +2 striking dancing dagger sheathed in a scabbard of keen edges (sharpening a blade to keen 1/day); and a medallion of thoughts stamped with the Sihedron (3/day mind reading).

  • B4 — the larder: rotting algae, a mangled 15-ft alligator, boggard skeletons crushed by terrible pressure — the god's leftovers.
  • B5 — Chamber of the Lynx: the mosaic of Sorshen's hallowed pet keeps residual power: touch it for a +2 status bonus to Charisma-based skill checks and Will saves for 1 hour (PF1e +5 insight). One touch drains it; it recharges in 24 hours, and any one creature can benefit once per month.
  • B6/B10/B14: the inter-level shafts (B6 up 40 ft to B10 — flooded, swimmable; B14 up 35 ft to B15 — dry, glass-smooth: @Check[type:athletics|dc:36] to Climb).
  • B7 — Surface Entrance: Ileosa's disintegrate-cut side door, screened by mangroves and Togomor's permanent illusory wall (@Check[type:perception|dc:30] to pick out the cut even once the illusion is pierced). One @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.boggardChamp0001]{boggard champion} stands sentry (Trivial · 0 XP @ L16) — and runs to rouse B8 on sight of intruders.
  • B8 — Boggard Quarters: the other five champions (Trivial · 0 XP @ L16) on a level wooden platform above the tilt (no slope penalty there). Alarmed, all six hunt the party. B9 — stores: smoked giant-gar fillets (genuinely delicious), 100+ lbs of swamp tubers — and boggard cranberry liquor: non-boggards @Check[type:fortitude|dc:30] or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} for hours (as a nauseating snare).
  • B11/B13: flooded chamber of red algae and hand-size yellow tadpoles (delicacies); the queen's drinking-water barrels on a floating platform (she will not drink swamp).
  • B12 — Crystal Tube: the main artery — down to the B2 globe, up in branching threads to the Pool's rim in B20 (the gaseous route to the treasury). Same impossible crystal as the walls.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.6, B15-B19 — p.386.

The air in this large room is warm and pleasant. The floor, walls, and ceiling are tiled with polished, rose-colored marble slabs. Climbing from the floor to the ceiling above and weaving in and out of the walls are dozens of thin crystal tubes. One shaft drops away in the floor to the southwest, while a second shaft rises up through the ceiling to the northeast.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ithierGylou00001]{Ithier (17)}
B15 — Great Hall: IthierLow · 60 XP @ L16

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ithierGylou00001]{Ithier}, last scarlet walker of Sorshen's court — drawn from Leng eleven millennia ago, bound here as immortal guardian, bought by Ileosa's promise to send it home. Mind fog round 1; a quickened confusion at a martial every round; greater invisibility round 2; dominate aimed at sending its victim to fetch the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.soultrapGem00001]{Soultrapping Gem} (B19). It reports the whole battle to Ileosa by telepathy, pursues anywhere but the flooded levels, and fights to the death — then climbs into B20 to die beside her if it survived the PCs here.

  • 4× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ileosasFury00001]{Ileosa's Fury (12)}
B16 — Ileosa's FuriesTrivial · 40 XP @ L16

Decabbara, Eveanie, Suishani, Verasia — the four @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ileosasFury00001]{erinyes furies} of the A90 contract, meditating among strap-fitted chairs on the room's level platform. On alert they hold for Ileosa's word that intruders reached this floor — and teleport to her side the instant she is attacked (their Everdawn Infusion ignores the interdiction). Thin them here in pairs, or meet all four at the Pool; sent to B2 against looters, they withdraw to the queen at half HP. Their tridents and ropes are the room's treasure.

B17 — Magical Workshop: Ileosa's lab for the Pool and her self-augmentations. On the bench: a half-completed set of ring gates — a PC crafter can finish them (@Check[type:crafting|dc:27] and the staged components, PF1e 10,000 gp — re-scale ~1,000 gp of materials, all present). B18 — Ileosa's Room (three continual flame globes; her quarters before the trance, little-used now): her second-string wardrobe and jewelry (PF1e 12,000 gp — ~1,200 gp) and a 65-lb magical harp hybridizing a harp of charming with pipes of sounding (PF1e 9,000 gp — ~900 gp).

This chamber smells of blood. The eastern portion contains a large square basin, the rim of which is decorated with a convoluted bas-relief that resembles a jumble of snakes and slugs. Within the basin is a drop of three feet to the surface of a pool of what appears to be blood. Numerous thin crystalline tubes extend out of the wall above the basin, pass through its rim, and emerge along the inner rim, each one protruding like a tiny spigot over the pool below.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.immortalIchor001]{Immortal Ichor (17)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.soultrapGem00001]{Soultrapping Gem (9)}
B19 — the Blood RepositoryLow · 60 XP @ L16

The Pool's old emergency reserve, kept warm and unclotted by faint necromancy — grown sentient around Sorshen's psychic residue: the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.immortalIchor001]{Immortal Ichor} rises in her vague shape (@Check[type:arcana|dc:38] or @Check[type:society|dc:38] to recognize the Runelord of Lust). It can no longer recharge the Pool; Ileosa keeps it, dreaming of absorbing it later. It guards the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.soultrapGem00001]{Soultrapping Gem} — the trap-the-soul sapphire inscribed with one PC's name — and orders any charmed victim to pick the stone up; a filled gem gets carried straight up to the queen.

pages.name: B20: THE EVERDAWN POOL — the Finale

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.6, B20 & Conclusion events — p.388.

This huge, lofty chamber is illuminated by braziers at the four corners. A soft light also filters from two very high oval windows on the southwestern wall. The twin windows are fitted with panes of blue crystal that filter in some light from outside. Below these crystal 'eyes,' a band of mosaics on the south wall forms a huge map of an ancient, unknown land. The map features a river valley with many villages, each one labeled in a strange language. To the northeast, a shaft drops away in the floor.

Yet the most unusual feature of the room floats and undulates at its center-an amorphous blob of blood, over thirty feet wide, hovers and ripples in the air. Shapes periodically form on its turbulent surface: faces, hands, buildings, and figures that last only long enough to melt back into the horrific mass. Dozens of thin crystal tubes extend from the upper walls of the room to a point just above the shifting mass of blood.

The blue ovals are Sorshen's eyes in the exterior relief; the mosaic maps Eurythnia at her height (the Grand Mastaba and an upright Sunken Queen are recognizable). The floating mass is the Everdawn Pool (major artifact — Appendices), glutted on the blood of thousands. Ileosa floats in semi-trance at its heart, neither breathing nor eating, each day's session burning a charge toward permanent youth. She notices entrants at once unless they are very stealthy — reaching the Pool's center unseen earns an automatic surprise round. Noticed, the Pool ripples into the Korvosa skyline, crumbles as if in an earthquake, and her shrieking face surfaces: no speech, only fury.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.queenIleosa00001]{ILEOSA (21)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.taniniverKaz0001]{Taniniver (17)}
  • 6× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.falseIleosa00001]{False Ileosa (9)}
  • + surviving @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ileosasFury00001]{Furies (12)} & @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ithierGylou00001]{Ithier (17)}
THE LAST BATTLEExtreme · 320 XP @ L16

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.queenIleosa00001]{QUEEN ILEOSA} (L21) unleashes her six stored @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.falseIleosa00001]{simulacrums}; every surviving @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ileosasFury00001]{Fury} teleports in; @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ithierGylou00001]{Ithier} climbs from below if it lives — and the Pool spends 5 charges to birth the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.taniniverKaz0001]{taniniver} of Kazavon (its every Strike inflicts blood veil; replaceable while charges last, one at a time, never beyond a mile from the Pool). An Extreme battle by design: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.serithtialSword1]{Serithtial} suppresses her regeneration, drains her with every cut, and alone can sunder the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.crownOfFangs0001]{Crown}. She fights to the death at the Pool — no retreat, no second speech. Killing (or decisively defeating) her destabilizes the Pool's stored charge: run the Rise of the Dragon immediately.

THE RISE OF THE DRAGON (CR 25 event): the Pool shudders; a talon lances out, then an immense skeletal wing — over 10 rounds the backlashing charge rebuilds Kazavon's true body (a blue dragon, formed skeleton-outward) while lightning arcs and iron chains whip through the room (@Check[type:religion|dc:20]: the chains are Zon-Kuthon's favor, transforming into flesh as they wrap him).

Each round, every PC in the room makes three saves — @Check[type:fortitude|dc:25] against the deafening roar or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}; @Check[type:reflex|dc:25|basic:true] against the lightning and chains — @Damage[5d6[bludgeoning]] plus @Damage[5d6[electricity]], halved on a success; @Check[type:will|dc:25] or fragments of thought are torn away — @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} (cumulative; PF1e dealt 2 Int/Wis/Cha drain per failure).

Stopping it: dispel evil or dispel law on the Pool with a counteract check against a DC of 30 halts it cold · antimagic field works only if it encompasses the entire Pool · disjunction-grade unmaking, wish, or miracle end it instantly · dispel magic does nothing · and the easiest: plunge @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.serithtialSword1]{Serithtial} into the Pool — it detonates in gore swallowed by searing radiance: no damage, the sword unharmed, but every creature in the room @Check[type:fortitude|dc:20] or permanently @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded}. Succeed by any road and Kazavon's seed dies forever.

If the 10 rounds elapse: the Pool goes dormant, spent — and @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch6KazavonReborn]{Kazavon Reborn} (CR 25 / ~L24) is on the field. The published guidance expects flight or glorious death, not victory.

WHAT IF ILEOSA WINS (driven off twice, or the ritual completes): every creature whose blood fed the Pool — all Gray Maidens, possibly PCs — dies in seconds, blood pouring out and evaporating; thousands of Korvosans perish, each buying her a year of vitality. Eternal, beautiful, and mad, she turns her enslaved survivors toward Cheliax — she craves adoration more than rule, and could become one of Avistan's great villains. (GM option: treat the completed ritual as a mythic ascension — keep her devil-bound chassis and add a trickster tier: surprise-strike and mythic spellcasting.) The campaign continues as resistance, in a darker world.

pages.name: Chapter Conclusion — Long Live the Queen

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.6, Immediate Repercussions — p.390.

Immediate repercussions: with Ileosa destroyed, the castle's surviving devils are drawn back to Hell (with @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.sermignattoDvl01]{Sermignatto}, if he lives); the Pool lies dormant; Korvosa steadies. The @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.crownOfFangs0001]{Crown of Fangs}: one strong blow from @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.serithtialSword1]{Serithtial} shatters it — but the fangs of Kazavon are indestructible and survive, quiescent and (for now) safe to handle. Their true destruction requires reuniting all seven relics, letting the dragon be reborn, and defeating him whole — the Continuing the Campaign hook.

The accounting: Neolandus and the rebels hold Castle Korvosa on the PCs' return; Cressida rebuilds the Guard; the Gray Maidens are disbanded and hiding; the Sable Company's restoration is tabled; cheering crowds meet the party at the gates. The rebellion's RP decides how bright the dawn is (under 5, the streets burned behind them — feed the Epidemic Clock its catastrophe tier). Epidemic Clock: with the harvest dead, blood veil's re-seeding ends — the clock empties over the denouement (the CHG-0011b payoff). Sabina's trial and pardon; Trinia's homecoming; Vencarlo passes the Blackjack mantle; Zellara and Venster are already home (A89). The Shoanti send riders — not for war.

Campaign complete. Epilogue and the optional post-finale arc: see the Appendices & Beyond journal (Continuing the Campaign — Kazavon Rises · Sorshen's Legacy · Rulers of Korvosa).
